{{Quote|My transport's at the spaceport. Mind you, I don't like any chatter on the way to Ussa. I don't need to know your life story, or you mine. Got that?|Weasy, describing his terms to Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="TDM" />}}
'''Weasy''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], who was well known for taking on any cargo, no questions asked, along with being an excellent reporter of information who didn't exaggerate. Shortly after the formation of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Weasy was at the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] in [[Ussa]], [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bellassa/Legends|Bellassa]], when the Empire shut down the spaceport searching for [[Ferus Olin]], a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] who had escaped a local prison. Following the shutdown, Weasy was forced to wait one week before being allowed to leave the Ussa spaceport. After being allowed to leave Bellassa, Weasey traveled to [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], a city on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. There, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] overheard Weasy talking about Ferus Olin, and upon learning Weasy was planning a supply run to Ussa, he hired the pilot to bring him along. After arriving in Ussa, Weasy discreetly wished Kenobi luck, and directed him to the passenger check in area before going to arrange docking for his ship. Weasy piloted a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] star yacht.<ref name="TDM" />

==Behind the scenes==
Weasy first appeared in the young readers novel, The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission, written by [[Jude Watson]], and released in [[April]] [[2005]]. Weasy would later receive a brief entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]], and released in [[December]] [[2008]]
